title: 'Getting this right': Labor unveils blueprint for AI
description: Anthony Albanese will draw parallels between the impact of artificial intelligence on society to the introduction of civil aviation in a major policy speech.
content:
The impact of artificial intelligence on society will be compared to the change brought by civil aviation 100 years ago as the prime minister unveils a new framework to deal with the fast-moving technology.

title: DeepMind CEO Says AGI Will Be Bigger Than Electricity or Fire
description: Demis Hassabis says artificial general intelligence is only a few years away and proposes a new U.S. standards body to test frontier AI models before release.

title: Karnataka To Establish India's First Govt-Driven Artificial Intelligence University: CM DK Shivakumar
description: Karnataka's Chief Minister DK Shivakumar has announced plans to establish India's first government-led Artificial Intelligence University and an AI Hub for research and development., Bengaluru News, Times Now
content:

title: George Lucas Is Pro AI, Which Shouldn’t Be a Surprise
description: The creator of 'Star Wars' and ILM thinks artificial intelligence is the future of filmmaking.
content: George Lucas never looks backwards. When he didn’t know how to make a shot he wanted in Star Wars, he invented the technology to do it. When he had a vision for the Star Wars prequels, he waited until technology caught up before he brought it to life... [3245 chars]

title: What Is Going on With NVIDIA Stock on Tuesday?
description: NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) stock rallied nearly 3% on Tuesday as global tech buyers flooded back into artificial intelligence leaders, backed by an aggressive $330 price target hike from KeyBanc.
content:
Nvidia remains well-positioned for AI data center growth, according to KeyBanc analyst John Vinh, who raised his price forecast despite some near-term ramp delays.

title: BCG sees wider AI execution gap in India as marketers prepare for agentic AI
description: Indian marketers embrace artificial intelligence with high ambition and investment. However, many companies mistake experimentation for true transformation and measurable impact. The gap between ambition and reality is wider in India than globally. Companies making progress redesign workflows and invest in data and employee upskilling.
content:
